input.content:
# Makefile for C Project
# Standard build configuration with common targets

# Compiler and flags
CC := gcc
CFLAGS := -Wall -Wextra -std=c99 -g
LDFLAGS := 
LDLIBS := 

# Directory structure
SRC_DIR := src
BUILD_DIR := build
BIN_DIR := $(BUILD_DIR)/bin
OBJ_DIR := $(BUILD_DIR)/obj
DEP_DIR := $(BUILD_DIR)/dep

# Source and output files
SOURCES := $(wildcard $(SRC_DIR)/*.c)
OBJECTS := $(patsubst $(SRC_DIR)/%.c,$(OBJ_DIR)/%.o,$(SOURCES))
DEPS := $(patsubst $(SRC_DIR)/%.c,$(DEP_DIR)/%.d,$(SOURCES))
TARGET := $(BIN_DIR)/app

# .PHONY targets
.PHONY: all build clean distclean install release help

# Default target
all: build

# Display help
help:
	@echo "Available build targets:"
	@echo "  make build      - Build the project"
	@echo "  make clean      - Remove object files and intermediates"
	@echo "  make distclean   - Remove all build artifacts"
	@echo "  make install    - Install the binary to /usr/local/bin"
	@echo "  make release    - Create a release tarball"
	@echo "  make help       - Display this help message"

# Build target
build: $(TARGET)

# Link the executable
$(TARGET): $(OBJECTS)
	@mkdir -p $(BIN_DIR)
	$(CC) $(LDFLAGS) -o $@ $^ $(LDLIBS)
	@echo "✓ Built: $@"

# Compile source files
$(OBJ_DIR)/%.o: $(SRC_DIR)/%.c
	@mkdir -p $(OBJ_DIR) $(DEP_DIR)
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -MMD -MP -MF $(DEP_DIR)/$*.d -c -o $@ $<
	@echo "✓ Compiled: $<"

# Include dependencies
-include $(DEPS)

# Install target
install: build
	@echo "Installing to /usr/local/bin/..."
	install -d /usr/local/bin
	install -m 755 $(TARGET) /usr/local/bin/app
	@echo "✓ Installed to /usr/local/bin/app"

# Release target
release: distclean build
	@echo "Creating release package..."
	@mkdir -p $(BUILD_DIR)/release
	strip $(TARGET)
	tar -czf $(BUILD_DIR)/release/app-release.tar.gz -C $(BIN_DIR) app
	@echo "✓ Release package created: $(BUILD_DIR)/release/app-release.tar.gz"

# Clean intermediate files
clean:
	@echo "Cleaning intermediate files..."
	rm -rf $(OBJ_DIR) $(DEP_DIR)
	@echo "✓ Clean complete"

# Deep clean - remove all build artifacts
distclean: clean
	@echo "Removing all build artifacts..."
	rm -rf $(BUILD_DIR)
	@echo "✓ Distclean complete"

# Print variables (for debugging)
print-%:
	@echo $* = $($*)

input.content:
# Build Configuration Guide

## Project Structure

```
myproject/
├── src/              # Source files
│   └── main.c       # Main program
├── build/           # Build artifacts (generated)
│   ├── bin/         # Compiled binaries
│   ├── obj/         # Object files
│   ├── dep/         # Dependency files
│   └── release/     # Release packages
├── Makefile         # Build configuration
└── BUILD.md         # This file
```

## Building the Project

### Standard Targets

#### `make build` (default)
Compiles the project and produces the executable.
```bash
make build
```

#### `make clean`
Removes intermediate build artifacts (object files, dependencies) but keeps the binary.
```bash
make clean
```

#### `make distclean`
Performs a complete cleanup, removing all build artifacts including binaries.
```bash
make distclean
```

#### `make install`
Builds the project and installs the binary to `/usr/local/bin/app`.
```bash
make install
```

#### `make release`
Creates a production release package with a stripped binary and tarball.
```bash
make release
```

#### `make help`
Displays all available build targets and their descriptions.
```bash
make help
```

## Compiler Configuration

- **Compiler**: GCC (gcc)
- **C Standard**: C99
- **Warnings**: `-Wall -Wextra` (all and extra warnings enabled)
- **Debug Info**: `-g` (included in all builds)

## Customization

To modify compiler flags, edit the `Makefile` and adjust these variables:

- `CC`: Change the C compiler
- `CFLAGS`: Modify compilation flags
- `LDFLAGS`: Linker flags
- `LDLIBS`: Link libraries

Example for optimization:
```make
CFLAGS := -Wall -Wextra -std=c99 -O2
```

## Running the Built Binary

After building:
```bash
./build/bin/app
```

## Output Example

```
$ make build
✓ Compiled: src/main.c
✓ Built: build/bin/app

$ ./build/bin/app
Hello, world!
```

## Dependency Management

The Makefile automatically generates and tracks dependencies using GCC's `-MMD -MP` flags. Dependencies are stored in `build/dep/` directory for proper incremental builds.

## Notes

- Builds are incremental: only modified files are recompiled
- The `build/` directory is safe to delete at any time
- No external build tools are required beyond standard Unix utilities
